summary: AI workflow agents for lending, compliance, and operations at banks and credit unions.
editor_note: The bet is that bank AI works only when workflow execution and human oversight ship together. Saris targets 50-step lending, compliance, and operations work, with official examples citing 70% task automation and 9.8x cost reduction per workflow. Unlike screenshot-driven RPA, its investor note describes native desktop bindings and self-healing agents. The risk is whether regulated buyers convert fast pilots into standard operating infrastructure.

product_evidence.claim: The official site says Saris automates multi-step lending, compliance, and operational workflows while keeping human approval and audit trails in place.
8VC describes native desktop bindings, self-healing agents, 50-plus-step lending workflows, and 90-day customer go-lives without dedicated implementation engineering.

market_context.primary_user: Banks and credit unions trying to automate manual back-office lending, compliance, and operations work.
market_context.differentiation: Banking-specific agent workflows that execute across legacy systems under human supervision.
market_context.why_now: The May 28, 2026 Series A led by 8VC funds expansion across more financial institutions and integrations.

risks.claim: The open question is whether regulated financial institutions can scale deployment without slow security and procurement cycles.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product and investor-source review.
